name: Whole Flounder with Herb Marinade
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the olive oil, fresh herbs, garlic, salt, and pepper. Set aside for up to 2 hours.
2. Rinse the fish well under cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
3. Using kitchen shears or a sharp knife, slash the thicker part of both fillets with 1 or 2 shallow diagonal cuts through the skin and into the meat.
4. Place the marinade in a small dish. Rub the remaining marinade all over the surface of the fish and into the slashes.
5. Set aside for 15 minutes to allow flavors to penetrate the fish.
6. Build a medium-hot fire in a charcoal grill. Oil the grill rack.
7. Place the fish, skin-side down, on the hot grate. Grill for 4 minutes, or until the skin releases easily from the grate.
8. Turn the fillets and baste with any remaining marinade. Continue grilling until a thin skewer inserted into the thickest part of the fish registers an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C), about 2-4 more minutes.
9. Serve hot.
Per Serving: 305 calories, 51 g protein, 10 g fat, 2 g saturated fat, 131 mg cholesterol, 327 mg sodium, no fiber, no carbohydrate.
